title: Customer Lifetime Value (LTV / CLV)
business_definition: LTV is the total revenue expected from a customer over their entire relationship. Simple LTV = ARPU / churn_rate. Predictive LTV uses cohort survival curves. A healthy business has LTV > 3x CAC.
when_to_use: which customer segments are most valuable
diagnostic_questions: Which customer segment has the highest LTV — by plan tier, region, acquisition channel?
Is LTV increasing or decreasing for newer cohorts vs older ones?
What drives LTV — tenure, expansion revenue, or referrals?
What is the payback period relative to LTV?

causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT cohort_month, months_since_start, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS retained, SUM(revenue) AS cohort_revenue FROM cohort_activity GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2
SELECT acquisition_channel, AVG(total_revenue) AS avg_ltv, AVG(cac) AS avg_cac, AVG(total_revenue) / NULLIF(AVG(cac), 0) AS ltv_cac_ratio FROM customer_summary GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 4 DESC

inflation_causes.cause: LTV projected using average churn ignores high early-churn — overstates expected tenure
inflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT DATEDIFF('month', signup_date, COALESCE(cancelled_at, CURRENT_DATE)) AS tenure_months, COUNT(*) FROM customers GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

deflation_causes.cause: Expansion revenue (upsells, add-ons) excluded from LTV calculation
deflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT order_type, SUM(amount) FROM orders GROUP BY 1

anti_patterns: Do not use simple ARPU / churn for high-growth companies — early cohorts are too small to represent steady state
Do not forget to discount future cash flows when computing LTV for long-horizon projections

sql_assets.simple_ltv: SELECT AVG(total_revenue) AS avg_ltv FROM (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY 1) t
